task: 'its still there' -- a screenshot of the deployed GitHub Pages site showed the Vihu Buddy placeholder still visible even though a real fix (display:none as a base CSS rule) had already shipped, and the dev footer's own build stamp confirmed the browser HAD picked up the newer HTML/JS -- a real cache mismatch, not a code regression: css/style.css and assets/fonts/fonts.css were linked with no version query string, so a cache could keep serving a stale copy of the same-URL stylesheet indefinitely, independent of the HTML document reloading fresh. Fix: both stylesheet link tags in index.html now carry a ?v=0153 query string -- a version bump changes the URL itself, forcing a genuine re-fetch regardless of the underlying cache mechanism. Established as a manual ritual going forward: bump ?v= on both stylesheet links alongside any future CSS-affecting ship, mirroring the existing build-info.json version-bump discipline. Verified via the existing Playwright regression suite. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/rendering change; confined to two link tags in index.html.
